rulings.Case Title: DAVID GRASSI VS. JULIEN SIGNES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Directing The Clerk'S Office Ot Honor Notice Of Lien And Disburse Funds Subject To The Lien;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, Line 1, CLAIMANT ANTHONY CICCOLINI'S Notice Of Motion For Order Directing The Clerk's Office Ot Honor Notice Of Lien And Disburse Funds Subject To The Lien Denied. Pursuant to CCP § 708.410, judgment creditor Ciccolini seeks to recover the $10,000 vexatious litigant bond that was posted by Mr.Grassi in this action, and now exonerated and discharged by Court order filed January 25, 2013. However, CCP 708.410 is not applicable to this situation. It permits a judgment creditor to obtain a lien on "any cause of action of such judgment debtor for money or property" or "the rights of such judgment debtor to money or property under any judgment subsequently procured in the action or proceeding." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO,ACTING VS. CURTIS LIND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, Line 22, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's Motion for Judgment as to All Claims and Cross-Claims between the City and Defendant Curtis Lind is granted. No triable issue of material fact exists for any cause of action. Defendant and Cross-Complainant Lind does not dispute any of the facts recited in Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Disputed Facts. Plaintiff has prevailed on the trespass and nuisance claims as a matter of law. Defendant does not have a valid sublease for any portion of the property at Pier 38. Defendant is interfering with Plaintiff's free use and enjoyment of the property, which constitutes a nuisance under Code of Civil Procedure § 3479. Defendant's cross-claims for breach of contract, intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, and negligent interference with prospective economic advantage are barred by the statute of limitations in California Government Code § 945.6. Defendant's cross-claim for inverse condemnation fails as a matter of law because it is undisputed that plaintiff has not taken or interfered with defendant's property and defendant has not submitted evidence to establish this claim. It is further ordered that defendant must vacate the property at Pier 38. Defendant is enjoined from occupying the City's property at Pier 38 and from mooring his vessel at Pier 38. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 17, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiff FRANCES SUSSNA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is DENIED as to defendant Jaclyn Fenton and GRANTED as to defendants Prime Old County L.P., Prime Old County Acquisition LLC, with INSTRUCTIONS. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED, Civil Code § 3295 contains no provision for sanctions to either party to this dispute. Defendants' request for a Protective Order is moot. Plaintiff seeks further responses from defendants Prime Old County L.P., Prime Old County Acquisition LLC and Jaclyn Fenton to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatory 1 and 2 which seek "to identify admissible documents and witnesses sufficient to establish their financial condition." (Plaintiff's P & A, 1:4-5). Following service of objections to the interrogatories a meet and confer process ensued which framed the issues but did not result in Plaintiff obtaining the requested information. Plaintiff's moving papers fail to establish with substantial probability that that the plaintiff will prevail on the claim for punitive damages. The plaintiff has the burden of proof. (Evid. Code §500; Adams v. Murakami (1991) 54 C3d 105, 119) Defendants are ORDERED to bring the requested information to trial, under seal, making it available to plaintiff only if the finder of fact determines that defendants were guilty of malice, oppression, or fraud. (Jabro v. Superior Court (2002) 95 CA4th 754, 758) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TERRENCE BARNUM VS. PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Discovery Responses And Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANTS PETER HEELAN, PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Discovery Responses And Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel Defendants' motion to compel is denied. The Court does not have jurisdiction to rule on the motion to compel and for sanctions. CCP §916 provides that "the perfecting of an appeal stays proceedings in the trial court¿upon the matters embraced therein or affected thereby¿" An appeal of an order denying arbitration affects the entire case. Prudential-Bache Securities, Inc. v. Superior Court (1988) 201 Cal.App.3d 924, 925. Thus, an appeal from the denial of a motion to compel arbitration automatically stays all further trial court proceedings on the merits. Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 190. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ARTI DENTERLEIN VS. JANE O'HARA GAMP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP, PAT ALLEY, SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the second, third, and sixth causes of action are sustained for failure to allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action (CCP §430.10(e)). As to the sixth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained as to Defendants Jane O'Hara Gamp and Pat Alley only without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not plead facts sufficient to establish that Gamp and Alley, as employees of San Francisco Law School, were parties to the contract, and therefore, they are not subject to the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. (See Cleary v. American Airlines, Inc. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 443, 456.) As to the second cause of action for violation of Business & Professions Code §17200, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has not plead facts sufficient to establish that members of the public are likely to be deceived. As to the third cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has not plead facts sufficient to allege a violation of a Federal or state constitutional right by defendants. The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is overruled; Plaintiff has alleged facts sufficient to state a claim. rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL COLLEGIATE STUDENT LOAN TRUST 2005-3 VS. ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Grant the motion quash service of summons. Plaintiff fails to meet its burden to show that Defendant was personally served on July 7, 2013. The return of the process server establishes a presumption, affecting the burden of producing evidence. Evidence Code § 647. However, here, Defendant declares under penalty of perjury that she was not home from July 6 to July 14, 2013 during which time the summons was allegedly served on her personally. It is not clear whether the proof of service was signed (the copy attached to the opposition has only "S" on the signature line). There is no declaration from the process server. =(302/MJM)

The return of the process server establishes a presumption, affecting the burden of producing evidence. Evidence Code § 647. However, here, Defendant declares under penalty of perjury that she was not home from July 6 to July 14, 2013 during which time the summons was allegedly served on her personally. It is not clear whether the proof of service was signed (the copy attached to the opposition has only "S" on the signature line). There is no declaration from the process server. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GRASSI VS CICCOLINI JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Noice Of Motion For Order Directing Clerk To Honor Levy And Turn Over Funds;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 17, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT ANTHONY J JR CICCOLINI'S Notice Of Motion For Order Directing Clerk To Honor Levy And Turn Over Funds Granted. No opposition filed. Judgment creditor Mr. Ciccolini is entitled to levy on the subject property pursuant to CCP §695.010(a). Mr. Ciccolini has served a notice of levy to the clerk's office for the $10,000 held in Case Number CGC-10-500974. The Clerk's Office did not object to the levy under CCP § 701.010(b). The $10,000 vexatious litigant bond that was posted by Mr. Grassi in this action was exonerated and discharged by Court order filed January 25, 2013. As such, it is subject to levy by Mr. Ciccolini, and the funds should be turned over to the Sheriff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
